top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic CRC 506 Gran Fondo Costa Rica's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
CRC 506 Gran Fondo Costa Rica's flat forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 87 miles.
At mile 60 of 87, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
CRC 506 Gran Fondo Costa Rica's flat rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
When someone attacks on flat, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of CRC 506 Gran Fondo Costa Rica. Train it until it feels boring.
At CRC 506 Gran Fondo Costa Rica, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At CRC 506 Gran Fondo Costa Rica, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
2-minute all-out efforts on flat. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what CRC 506 Gran Fondo Costa Rica's flat actually does to you.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 87 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 87 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for CRC 506 Gran Fondo Costa Rica’s 87 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at CRC 506 Gran Fondo Costa Rica requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on flat to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, Gravel Specific, and Durability to prepare you for 87 miles of flat in Jacó, Garabito, Puntarenas Province, Costa Rica.
